product: Selectively replace data with INSERT REPLACE ON (Public Preview)
tagline: INSERT REPLACE ON is now in Public Preview for Databricks Runtime 17.1.
what_it_does: INSERT REPLACE ON is now in Public Preview for Databricks Runtime 17.1. This SQL command replaces part of the table with the result of a query. Rows are replaced when they match the user defined condition.
why_it_matters: Parameters common table expression Common table expressions (CTE) are one or more named queries which can be reused multiple times within the main query block to avoid repeated computations or to improve readability of complex, nested queries.

capabilities.title: Without a partitionspec the table
capabilities.desc: Without a partitionspec the table is truncated before inserting the first row.

capabilities.title: Otherwise, all partitions matching the
capabilities.desc: Otherwise, all partitions matching the partitionspec are truncated before inserting the first row.

capabilities.title: If this command omits a
capabilities.desc: If this command omits a column, Databricks SQL assigns the corresponding default value instead.

capabilities.title: If the target table schema
capabilities.desc: If the target table schema does not define any default value for the inserted column, Databricks SQL assigns NULL if the column…

key_takeaway: INSERT REPLACE ON is now in Public Preview for Databricks Runtime 17.1.
